Patch Set 2:

I haven't looked at the code for this yet, so I'm not sure if it is possible, but what about always setting the COS lock on a distributed DNE operation and always enforcing COS locks, but not setting it enforcing the COS lock for local operations?  That puts the complexity in the place it is needed - in DNE operations, and avoids overhead for the non-DNE case.
Uploaded patch set 3.

Patch Set 3:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 3:

Andreas, the way you proposed is similar to this, because an operation doesn't know whether it will modify remote object until it tries to enqueue PW/EX remote lock, so after that it can know whether it should compare with COS lock when enqueueing local lock, and the same it needs to relock from start.

parents.subject: LU-3538 dne: sync on cancel for cross-MDT lock

commit.subject: LU-3538 cos: CoS can take effect for cross-MDT op only
commit.message:
LU-3538 cos: CoS can take effect for cross-MDT op only

On DNE, PW/EX local lock will be saved as COS lock, but such lock
will be ignored in compatilibity check by default, unless it's
required, there are two situations:
1. when distributed transaction locks local object, it will check
   compatibility against COS locks.
2. when distributed transaction enqueues cross-MDT lock, it will
   check compatiblity against COS locks.

This patch contains following changes:
* on DNE, PW/EX lock is converted to COS and saved like before even
  when COS is not enabled.
* above COS locks will be ignored in lock compatibility check by
  default, so for single MDT COS won't take effect. But if operation
  finds itself may modify remote MDT object, it will lock all local
  locks with COS checked.
* cross-MDT lock will always check against COS locks.
* if operation is reint, it will check in each mdt_object_lock()
  to see whether current lock is cross-MDT lock, if so, it will
  relock from start to ensure all local locks are taken with COS
  checked.

Signed-off-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Change-Id: I261726279674dfc1b2d44cb34a162b6543e8cb4e
